NKJV.1: In You, O LORD, I put my trust; Let me never be put to shame.
NKJV.2: Deliver me in Your righteousness, and cause me to escape;  Incline Your ear to me, and save me.
NKJV.3: Be my strong refuge, To which I may resort continually; You have given the commandment to save me, For You are my rock and my fortress.
NKJV.4: Deliver me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, Out of the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
NKJV.5: For You are my hope, O Lord GOD; You are my trust from my youth.
NKJV.6: By You I have been upheld from birth; You are He who took me out of my mother’s womb. My praise shall be continually of You.
NKJV.7: I have become as a wonder to many, But You are my strong refuge.
NKJV.8: Let my mouth be filled with Your praise And with Your glory all the day.
NKJV.9: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; Do not forsake me when my strength fails.
NKJV.10: For my enemies speak against me; And those who lie in wait for my life take counsel together,
NKJV.11: Saying, “God has forsaken him; Pursue and take him, for there is none to deliver him.”
NKJV.12: O God, do not be far from me; O my God, make haste to help me!
NKJV.13: Let them be confounded and consumed Who are adversaries of my life; Let them be covered with reproach and dishonor Who seek my hurt.
NKJV.14: But I will hope continually, And will praise You yet more and more.
NKJV.15: My mouth shall tell of Your righteousness And Your salvation all the day, For I do not know their limits.
NKJV.16: I will go in the strength of the Lord GOD; I will make mention of Your righteousness, of Yours only.
NKJV.17: O God, You have taught me from my youth; And to this day I declare Your wondrous works.
NKJV.18: Now also when I am old and grayheaded, O God, do not forsake me, Until I declare Your strength to this generation, Your power to everyone who is to come.
NKJV.19: Also Your righteousness, O God, is very high, You who have done great things; O God, who is like You?
NKJV.20: You, who have shown me great and severe troubles, Shall revive me again, And bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
NKJV.21: You shall increase my greatness, And comfort me on every side.
NKJV.22: Also with the lute I will praise You— And Your faithfulness, O my God! To You I will sing with the harp, O Holy One of Israel.
NKJV.23: My lips shall greatly rejoice when I sing to You, And my soul, which You have redeemed.
NKJV.24: My tongue also shall talk of Your righteousness all the day long; For they are confounded, For they are brought to shame Who seek my hurt.

ESV.1: In you, O LORD, do I take refuge; let me never be put to shame!
ESV.2: In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; incline your ear to me, and save me!
ESV.3: Be to me a rock of refuge, to which I may continually come; you have given the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.
ESV.4: Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the grasp of the unjust and cruel man.
ESV.5: For you, O Lord, are my hope, my trust, O LORD, from my youth.
ESV.6: Upon you I have leaned from before my birth; you are he who took me from my mother's womb. My praise is continually of you.
ESV.7: I have been as a portent to many, but you are my strong refuge.
ESV.8: My mouth is filled with your praise, and with your glory all the day.
ESV.9: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; forsake me not when my strength is spent.
ESV.10: For my enemies speak concerning me; those who watch for my life consult together
ESV.11: and say, “God has forsaken him; pursue and seize him, for there is none to deliver him.”
ESV.12: O God, be not far from me; O my God, make haste to help me!
ESV.13: May my accusers be put to shame and consumed; with scorn and disgrace may they be covered who seek my hurt.
ESV.14: But I will hope continually and will praise you yet more and more.
ESV.15: My mouth will tell of your righteous acts, of your deeds of salvation all the day, for their number is past my knowledge.
ESV.16: With the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD I will come; I will remind them of your righteousness, yours alone.
ESV.17: O God, from my youth you have taught me, and I still proclaim your wondrous deeds.
ESV.18: So even to old age and gray hairs, O God, do not forsake me, until I proclaim your might to another generation, your power to all those to come.
ESV.19: Your righteousness, O God, reaches the high heavens. You who have done great things, O God, who is like you?
ESV.20: You who have made me see many troubles and calamities will revive me again; from the depths of the earth you will bring me up again.
ESV.21: You will increase my greatness and comfort me again.
ESV.22: I will also praise you with the harp for your faithfulness, O my God; I will sing praises to you with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.
ESV.23: My lips will shout for joy, when I sing praises to you; my soul also, which you have redeemed.
ESV.24: And my tongue will talk of your righteous help all the day long, for they have been put to shame and disappointed who sought to do me hurt.

NASB.1: In You, O LORD, I have taken refuge; Let me never be ashamed.
NASB.2: In Your righteousness deliver me and rescue me; Incline Your ear to me and save me.
NASB.3: Be to me a rock of habitation to which I may continually come; You have given commandment to save me, For You are my rock and my fortress.
NASB.4: Rescue me, O my God, out of the hand of the wicked, Out of the grasp of the wrongdoer and ruthless man,
NASB.5: For You are my hope; O Lord GOD, [You are] my confidence from my youth.
NASB.6: By You I have been sustained from [my] birth; You are He who took me from my mother's womb; My praise is continually of You.
NASB.7: I have become a marvel to many, For You are my strong refuge.
NASB.8: My mouth is filled with Your praise And with Your glory all day long.
NASB.9: Do not cast me off in the time of old age; Do not forsake me when my strength fails.
NASB.10: For my enemies have spoken against me; And those who watch for my life have consulted together,
NASB.11: Saying, 'God has forsaken him; Pursue and seize him, for there is no one to deliver.'
NASB.12: O God, do not be far from me; O my God, hasten to my help!
NASB.13: Let those who are adversaries of my soul be ashamed [and] consumed; Let them be covered with reproach and dishonor, who seek to injure me.
NASB.14: But as for me, I will hope continually, And will praise You yet more and more.
NASB.15: My mouth shall tell of Your righteousness [And] of Your salvation all day long; For I do not know the sum [of them].
NASB.16: I will come with the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD; I will make mention of Your righteousness, Yours alone.
NASB.17: O God, You have taught me from my youth, And I still declare Your wondrous deeds.
NASB.18: And even when [I am] old and gray, O God, do not forsake me, Until I declare Your strength to [this] generation, Your power to all who are to come.
NASB.19: For Your righteousness, O God, [reaches] to the heavens, You who have done great things; O God, who is like You?
NASB.20: You who have shown me many troubles and distresses Will revive me again, And will bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
NASB.21: May You increase my greatness And turn [to] comfort me.
NASB.22: I will also praise You with a harp, [Even] Your truth, O my God; To You I will sing praises with the lyre, O Holy One of Israel.
NASB.23: My lips will shout for joy when I sing praises to You; And my soul, which You have redeemed.
NASB.24: My tongue also will utter Your righteousness all day long; For they are ashamed, for they are humiliated who seek my hurt.

NLT.1:
O LORD, I have come to you for protection;
don’t let me be disgraced.
NLT.2:
Save me and rescue me,
for you do what is right.
Turn your ear to listen to me,
and set me free.
NLT.3:
Be my rock of safety
where I can always hide.
Give the order to save me,
for you are my rock and my fortress.
NLT.4:
My God, rescue me from the power of the wicked,
from the clutches of cruel oppressors.
NLT.5:
O Lord, you alone are my hope.
I’ve trusted you, O LORD, from childhood.
NLT.6:
Yes, you have been with me from birth;
from my mother’s womb you have cared for me.
No wonder I am always praising you!
NLT.7:
My life is an example to many,
because you have been my strength and protection.
NLT.8:
That is why I can never stop praising you;
I declare your glory all day long.
NLT.9:
And now, in my old age, don’t set me aside.
Don’t abandon me when my strength is failing.
NLT.10:
For my enemies are whispering against me.
They are plotting together to kill me.
NLT.11:
They say, “God has abandoned him.
Let’s go and get him,
for no one will help him now.”
NLT.12:
O God, don’t stay away.
My God, please hurry to help me.
NLT.13:
Bring disgrace and destruction on my accusers.
Humiliate and shame those who want to harm me.
NLT.14:
But I will keep on hoping for your help;
I will praise you more and more.
NLT.15:
I will tell everyone about your righteousness.
All day long I will proclaim your saving power,
though I am not skilled with words.
NLT.16:
I will praise your mighty deeds, O Sovereign LORD.
I will tell everyone that you alone are just.
NLT.17:
O God, you have taught me from my earliest childhood,
and I constantly tell others about the wonderful things you do.
NLT.18:
Now that I am old and gray,
do not abandon me, O God.
Let me proclaim your power to this new generation,
your mighty miracles to all who come after me.
NLT.19:
Your righteousness, O God, reaches to the highest heavens.
You have done such wonderful things.
Who can compare with you, O God?
NLT.20:
You have allowed me to suffer much hardship,
but you will restore me to life again
and lift me up from the depths of the earth.
NLT.21:
You will restore me to even greater honor
and comfort me once again.
NLT.22:
Then I will praise you with music on the harp,
because you are faithful to your promises, O my God.
I will sing praises to you with a lyre,
O Holy One of Israel.
NLT.23:
I will shout for joy and sing your praises,
for you have ransomed me.
NLT.24:
I will tell about your righteous deeds
all day long,
for everyone who tried to hurt me
has been shamed and humiliated.

WEB.1: In you, Yahweh, I take refuge. Never let me be disappointed.
WEB.2: Deliver me in your righteousness, and rescue me. Turn your ear to me, and save me.
WEB.3: Be to me a rock of refuge to which I may always go. Give the command to save me, for you are my rock and my fortress.
WEB.4: Rescue me, my God, from the hand of the wicked, from the hand of the unrighteous and cruel man.
WEB.5: For you are my hope, Lord Yahweh; my confidence from my youth.
WEB.6: I have relied on you from the womb. You are he who took me out of my mother’s womb. I will always praise you.
WEB.7: I am a marvel to many, but you are my strong refuge.
WEB.8: My mouth shall be filled with your praise, with your honor all day long.
WEB.9: Don’t reject me in my old age. Don’t forsake me when my strength fails.
WEB.10: For my enemies talk about me. Those who watch for my soul conspire together,
WEB.11: saying, “God has forsaken him. Pursue and take him, for no one will rescue him.”
WEB.12: God, don’t be far from me. My God, hurry to help me.
WEB.13: Let my accusers be disappointed and consumed. Let them be covered with disgrace and scorn who want to harm me.
WEB.14: But I will always hope, and will add to all of your praise.
WEB.15: My mouth will tell about your righteousness, and of your salvation all day, though I don’t know its full measure.
WEB.16: I will come with the mighty acts of the Lord Yahweh. I will make mention of your righteousness, even of yours alone.
WEB.17: God, you have taught me from my youth. Until now, I have declared your wondrous works.
WEB.18: Yes, even when I am old and gray-haired, God, don’t forsake me, until I have declared your strength to the next generation, your might to everyone who is to come.
WEB.19: Your righteousness also, God, reaches to the heavens; you have done great things. God, who is like you?
WEB.20: You, who have shown us many and bitter troubles, you will let me live. You will bring us up again from the depths of the earth.
WEB.21: Increase my honor, and comfort me again.
WEB.22: I will also praise you with the harp for your faithfulness, my God. I sing praises to you with the lyre, Holy One of Israel.
WEB.23: My lips shall shout for joy! My soul, which you have redeemed, sings praises to you!
WEB.24: My tongue will also talk about your righteousness all day long, for they are disappointed, and they are confounded, who want to harm me.

CSB17.1:
LORD, I seek refuge in you;
let me never be disgraced. ⓕ
CSB17.2:
In your justice, rescue and deliver me;
listen closely to me and save me. ⓖ
CSB17.3:
Be a rock of refuge for me,
where I can always go.
Give the command to save me,
for you are my rock and fortress. ⓗ
CSB17.4:
Deliver me, my God, from the power of the wicked,
from the grasp of the unjust and oppressive. ⓘ
CSB17.5:
For you are my hope, Lord GOD,
my confidence from my youth. ⓙ
CSB17.6:
I have leaned on you from birth;
you took me from my mother’s womb. ⓚ
My praise is always about you. ⓛ
CSB17.7:
I am like a miraculous sign to many,
and you are my strong refuge. ⓜ
CSB17.8:
My mouth is full of praise
and honor to you all day long. ⓝ
CSB17.9:
Don’t discard me in my old age.
As my strength fails, do not abandon me. ⓐ
CSB17.10:
For my enemies talk about me,
and those who spy on me plot together, ⓑ
CSB17.11:
saying, “God has abandoned him;
chase him and catch him,
for there is no one to rescue him.” ⓒ
CSB17.12:
God, do not be far from me;
my God, hurry to help me. ⓓ
CSB17.13:
May my adversaries be disgraced and destroyed;
may those who intend to harm me
be covered with disgrace and humiliation. ⓔ
CSB17.14:
But I will hope continually
and will praise you more and more. ⓕ
CSB17.15:
My mouth will tell about your righteousness
and your salvation all day long,
though I cannot sum them up. ⓖ
CSB17.16:
I come because of the mighty acts of the Lord GOD;
I will proclaim your righteousness, yours alone. ⓗ
CSB17.17:
God, you have taught me from my youth,
and I still proclaim your wondrous works. ⓘ
CSB17.18:
Even while I am old and gray,
God, do not abandon me, ⓙ
while I proclaim your power
to another generation,
your strength to all who are to come. ⓚ
CSB17.19:
Your righteousness reaches the heights, God,
you who have done great things;
God, who is like you? ⓛ
CSB17.20:
You caused me to experience
many troubles and misfortunes, ⓜ
but you will revive me again. ⓝ
You will bring me up again,
even from the depths of the earth. ⓐ
CSB17.21:
You will increase my honor
and comfort me once again. ⓑ
CSB17.22:
Therefore, I will praise you with a harp
for your faithfulness, my God;
I will sing to you with a lyre,
Holy One of Israel. ⓒ
CSB17.23:
My lips will shout for joy
when I sing praise to you
because you have redeemed me. ⓓ
CSB17.24:
Therefore, my tongue will proclaim
your righteousness all day long,
for those who intend to harm me
will be disgraced and confounded. ⓔ

AMP.1:
In you, O LORD, I have put my trust and confidently taken refuge;
Let me never be put to shame.
AMP.2:
In Your righteousness deliver me and rescue me;
Incline Your ear to me and save me.
AMP.3:
Be to me a rock of refuge and a sheltering stronghold to which I may continually come;
You have given the commandment to save me,
For You are my rock and my fortress.
AMP.4:
Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked (godless),
From the grasp of the unrighteous and ruthless man.
AMP.5:
For You are my hope;
O Lord GOD, You are my trust and the source of my confidence from my youth.
AMP.6:
Upon You have I relied and been sustained from my birth;
You are He who took me from my mother's womb and You have been my benefactor from that day.
My praise is continually of You.
AMP.7:
I am as a wonder to many,
For You are my strong refuge.
AMP.8:
My mouth is filled with Your praise
And with Your glory all day long.
AMP.9:
Do not cast me off nor send me away in the time of old age;
Do not abandon me when my strength fails and I am weak.
AMP.10:
For my enemies have spoken against me;
Those who watch for my life have consulted together,
AMP.11:
Saying, "God has abandoned him;
Pursue and seize him, for there is no one to rescue him."
AMP.12:
O God, do not be far from me;
O my God, come quickly to help me!
AMP.13:
Let those who attack my life be ashamed and consumed;
Let them be covered with reproach and dishonor, who seek to injure me.
AMP.14:
But as for me, I will wait and hope continually,
And will praise You yet more and more.
AMP.15:
My mouth shall tell of Your righteousness
And of Your [deeds of] salvation all day long,
For their number is more than I know.
AMP.16:
I will come with the mighty acts of the Lord GOD [and in His strength];
I will make mention of Your righteousness, Yours alone.
AMP.17:
O God, You have taught me from my youth,
And I still declare Your wondrous works and miraculous deeds.
AMP.18:
And even when I am old and gray-headed, O God, do not abandon me,
Until I declare Your [mighty] strength to this generation,
Your power to all who are to come.
AMP.19:
Your righteousness, O God, reaches to the [height of the] heavens,
You who have done great things;
O God, who is like You, [who is Your equal]?
AMP.20:
You who have shown me many troubles and distresses
Will revive and renew me again,
And will bring me up again from the depths of the earth.
AMP.21:
May You increase my greatness (honor)
And turn to comfort me.
AMP.22:
I will also praise You with the harp,
Your truth and faithfulness, O my God;
To You I will sing praises with the lyre,
O Holy One of Israel.
AMP.23:
My lips will shout for joy when I sing praises to You,
And my soul, which You have redeemed.
AMP.24:
My tongue also will speak of Your righteousness all day long;
For they are ashamed, for they are humiliated who seek my injury.

MSG.1:
I run for dear life to God,
I'll never live to regret it.
MSG.2:
Do what you do so well:
get me out of this mess and up on my feet.
Put your ear to the ground and listen,
give me space for salvation.
MSG.3:
Be a guest room where I can retreat;
you said your door was always open!
You're my salvation—my vast, granite fortress.
MSG.4:
My God, free me from the grip of Wicked,
from the clutch of Bad and Bully.
MSG.5:
You keep me going when times are tough—
my bedrock, God, since my childhood.
MSG.6:
I've hung on you from the day of my birth,
the day you took me from the cradle;
I'll never run out of praise.
MSG.7:
Many gasp in alarm when they see me,
but you take me in stride.
MSG.8:
Just as each day brims with your beauty,
my mouth brims with praise.
MSG.9:
But don't turn me out to pasture when I'm old
or put me on the shelf when I can't pull my weight.
MSG.10:
My enemies are talking behind my back,
watching for their chance to knife me.
MSG.11:
The gossip is: "God has abandoned him.
Pounce on him now; no one will help him."
MSG.12:
God, don't just watch from the sidelines.
Come on! Run to my side!
MSG.13:
My accusers—make them lose face.
Those out to get me—make them look
Like idiots,
MSG.14:
while I stretch out, reaching for you,
and daily add praise to praise.
MSG.15:
I'll write the book on your righteousness,
talk up your salvation the livelong day,
never run out of good things to write or say.
MSG.16:
I come in the power of the Lord God,
I post signs marking his right-of-way.
MSG.17:
You got me when I was an unformed youth,
God, and taught me everything I know.
Now I'm telling the world your wonders;
MSG.18:
I'll keep at it until I'm old and gray.
God, don't walk off and leave me
until I get out the news
Of your strong right arm to this world,
news of your power to the world yet to come,
MSG.19:
Your famous and righteous
ways, O God.
God, you've done it all!
Who is quite like you?
MSG.20:
You, who made me stare trouble in the face,
Turn me around;
Now let me look life in the face.
I've been to the bottom;
Bring me up,
MSG.21:
streaming with honors;
turn to me, be tender to me,
MSG.22:
And I'll take up the lute and thank you
to the tune of your faithfulness, God.
I'll make music for you on a harp,
Holy One of Israel.
MSG.23:
When I open up in song to you,
I let out lungsful of praise,
my rescued life a song.
MSG.24:
All day long I'm chanting
about you and your righteous ways,
While those who tried to do me in
slink off looking ashamed.

NIV.1:
Psalm 71
In you, O Lord, I have taken refuge;
let me never be put to shame.
NIV.2:
Rescue me and deliver me in your righteousness;
turn your ear to me and save me.
NIV.3:
Be my rock of refuge,
to which I can always go;
give the command to save me,
for you are my rock and my fortress.
NIV.4:
Deliver me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked,
from the grasp of evil and cruel men.
NIV.5:
For you have been my hope, O Sovereign Lord,
my confidence since my youth.
NIV.6:
From birth I have relied on you;
you brought me forth from my mother’s womb.
I will ever praise you.
NIV.7:
I have become like a portent to many,
but you are my strong refuge.
NIV.8:
My mouth is filled with your praise,
declaring your splendor all day long.
NIV.9:
Do not cast me away when I am old;
do not forsake me when my strength is gone.
NIV.10:
For my enemies speak against me;
those who wait to kill me conspire together.
NIV.11:
They say, “God has forsaken him;
pursue him and seize him,
for no one will rescue him.”
NIV.12:
Be not far from me, O God;
come quickly, O my God, to help me.
NIV.13:
May my accusers perish in shame;
may those who want to harm me
be covered with scorn and disgrace.
NIV.14:
But as for me, I will always have hope;
I will praise you more and more.
NIV.15:
My mouth will tell of your righteousness,
of your salvation all day long,
though I know not its measure.
NIV.16:
I will come and proclaim your mighty acts, O Sovereign Lord;
I will proclaim your righteousness, yours alone.
NIV.17:
Since my youth, O God, you have taught me,
and to this day I declare your marvelous deeds.
NIV.18:
Even when I am old and gray,
do not forsake me, O God,
till I declare your power to the next generation,
your might to all who are to come.
NIV.19:
Your righteousness reaches to the skies, O God,
you who have done great things.
Who, O God, is like you?
NIV.20:
Though you have made me see troubles, many and bitter,
you will restore my life again;
from the depths of the earth
you will again bring me up.
NIV.21:
You will increase my honor
and comfort me once again.
NIV.22:
I will praise you with the harp
for your faithfulness, O my God;
I will sing praise to you with the lyre,
O Holy One of Israel.
NIV.23:
My lips will shout for joy
when I sing praise to you—
I, whom you have redeemed.
NIV.24:
My tongue will tell of your righteous acts
all day long,
for those who wanted to harm me
have been put to shame and confusion.

NRSVCE.1:
In you, O LORD, I take refuge;
let me never be put to shame.
NRSVCE.2:
In your righteousness deliver me and rescue me;
incline your ear to me and save me.
NRSVCE.3:
Be to me a rock of refuge,
a strong fortress, to save me,
for you are my rock and my fortress.
NRSVCE.4:
Rescue me, O my God, from the hand of the wicked,
from the grasp of the unjust and cruel.
NRSVCE.5:
For you, O Lord, are my hope,
my trust, O LORD, from my youth.
NRSVCE.6:
Upon you I have leaned from my birth;
it was you who took me from my mother’s womb.
My praise is continually of you.
NRSVCE.7:
I have been like a portent to many,
but you are my strong refuge.
NRSVCE.8:
My mouth is filled with your praise,
and with your glory all day long.
NRSVCE.9:
Do not cast me off in the time of old age;
do not forsake me when my strength is spent.
NRSVCE.10:
For my enemies speak concerning me,
and those who watch for my life consult together.
NRSVCE.11:
They say, “Pursue and seize that person
whom God has forsaken,
for there is no one to deliver.”
NRSVCE.12:
O God, do not be far from me;
O my God, make haste to help me!
NRSVCE.13:
Let my accusers be put to shame and consumed;
let those who seek to hurt me
be covered with scorn and disgrace.
NRSVCE.14:
But I will hope continually,
and will praise you yet more and more.
NRSVCE.15:
My mouth will tell of your righteous acts,
of your deeds of salvation all day long,
though their number is past my knowledge.
NRSVCE.16:
I will come praising the mighty deeds of the Lord GOD,
I will praise your righteousness, yours alone.
NRSVCE.17:
O God, from my youth you have taught me,
and I still proclaim your wondrous deeds.
NRSVCE.18:
So even to old age and gray hairs,
O God, do not forsake me,
until I proclaim your might
to all the generations to come.
Your power
NRSVCE.19:
and your righteousness, O God,
reach the high heavens.
You who have done great things,
O God, who is like you?
NRSVCE.20:
You who have made me see many troubles and calamities
will revive me again;
from the depths of the earth
you will bring me up again.
NRSVCE.21:
You will increase my honor,
and comfort me once again.
NRSVCE.22:
I will also praise you with the harp
for your faithfulness, O my God;
I will sing praises to you with the lyre,
O Holy One of Israel.
NRSVCE.23:
My lips will shout for joy
when I sing praises to you;
my soul also, which you have rescued.
NRSVCE.24:
All day long my tongue will talk of your righteous help,
for those who tried to do me harm
have been put to shame, and disgraced.

NET.1: In you, O LORD, I have taken shelter! Never let me be humiliated!
NET.2: Vindicate me by rescuing me! Listen to me! Deliver me!
NET.3: Be my protector and refuge, a stronghold where I can be safe! For you are my high ridge and my stronghold.
NET.4: My God, rescue me from the power of the wicked, from the hand of the cruel oppressor!
NET.5: For you give me confidence, O Lord; O LORD, I have trusted in you since I was young.
NET.6: I have leaned on you since birth; you pulled me from my mother’s womb. I praise you continually.
NET.7: Many are appalled when they see me, but you are my secure shelter.
NET.8: I praise you constantly and speak of your splendor all day long.
NET.9: Do not reject me in my old age! When my strength fails, do not abandon me!
NET.10: For my enemies talk about me; those waiting for a chance to kill me plot my demise.
NET.11: They say, “God has abandoned him. Run and seize him, for there is no one who will rescue him!”
NET.12: O God, do not remain far away from me! My God, hurry and help me!
NET.13: May my accusers be humiliated and defeated! May those who want to harm me be covered with scorn and disgrace!
NET.14: As for me, I will wait continually, and will continue to praise you.
NET.15: I will tell about your justice, and all day long proclaim your salvation, though I cannot fathom its full extent.
NET.16: I will come and tell about the mighty acts of the sovereign LORD. I will proclaim your justice — yours alone.
NET.17: O God, you have taught me since I was young, and I am still declaring your amazing deeds.
NET.18: Even when I am old and gray, O God, do not abandon me, until I tell the next generation about your strength, and those coming after me about your power.
NET.19: Your justice, O God, extends to the skies above; you have done great things. O God, who can compare to you?
NET.20: Though you have allowed me to experience much trouble and distress, revive me once again! Bring me up once again from the depths of the earth!
NET.21: Raise me to a position of great honor! Turn and comfort me!
NET.22: I will express my thanks to you with a stringed instrument, praising your faithfulness, O my God! I will sing praises to you accompanied by a harp, O Holy One of Israel!
NET.23: My lips will shout for joy! Yes, I will sing your praises! I will praise you when you rescue me!
NET.24: All day long my tongue will also tell about your justice, for those who want to harm me will be embarrassed and ashamed.
